title: Intensely Yeasty Venezuelan Plantain Fritters
description: A paleo-friendly Venezuelan dish featuring rum and fried plantains, perfect for a midnight craving. These pillowy fritters are made with yeast and cooked on a tava griddle.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the mashed plantains, yeast, and rum, and let it sit for 10 minutes.
Add the coconut flour, eggs, coconut oil, salt, and sugar to the bowl, and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Heat a tava griddle over medium heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the griddle.
Flatten the batter slightly with a spatula.
Cook the fritters for 4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown.
Let the fritters cool completely on a wire rack before storing them in an airtight container.
